cross_scene_arcs.title: The Tool and the Trade: Mastery of Criminal Craft
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chris evolves from acquiring specialized tools to executing high-stakes technical breaches, demonstrating a focused progression in his criminal expertise. The hollow core drill becomes a symbol of his professional identity, culminating in its use during critical heist operations.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chris prepares for criminal work by purchasing a hollow core drill, aligning with his role as a safe-cracker.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chris actively uses precision drilling to breach a vault, marking full operational integration into the heist.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The reliance on technical tools heightens risk if equipment fails or draws attention, a vulnerability that remains unmitigated.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fracture and Fragility: The Collapse of Chris's Domestic Life
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chris's relationship with Charlene deteriorates under the strain of his criminal life, moving from emotional detachment and threats to a fragile, failed attempt at reconciliation, revealing his internal conflict between family and crime.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chris threatens Charlene to prevent her from leaving, severing emotional trust.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chris attempts a small gesture of connection (paying the bill), but emotional distance persists, signaling irreversible damage.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The future of Chris and Charlene's relationship—and the safety of their son Dominick—remains uncertain and unstable.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Planning to Blood: Descent into Violent Commitment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chris transitions from logistical support to direct violence, marking his irreversible entry into the crew's violent core. His role shifts from technician to aggressor, culminating in physical assault during the bank heist.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chris joins Cerrito in a violent ambush, committing to high-risk criminal action.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chris physically attacks and disarms a guard, asserting control through force and completing his transformation into an active perpetrator.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The psychological toll of sustained violence and the risk of future confrontations remain unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Lone Path: Defiance of Crew Unity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chris shifts from a team-oriented operative to an independent actor, resisting group decisions and asserting personal control over his fate, especially in evasion and escape, signaling a growing divergence from the crew's collective strategy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chris commits to the heist despite funding loss and surveillance, affirming loyalty but under personal risk assessment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chris rejects the group escape plan, choosing to drive alone and severing alignment with Neil and Nate's coordinated exit.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His solo path increases exposure and reduces mutual support, raising doubts about long-term survival and evasion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Edge of Detection: Navigating Surveillance and Paranoia
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chris moves from unawareness to active evasion of law enforcement, navigating an escalating environment of surveillance and operational compromise, blending tactical awareness with growing paranoia.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chris learns of possible police surveillance and Cerrito's disappearance, triggering suspicion.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chris successfully evades his tail and confirms the alarm bypass, operating off-grid and under full stealth.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The extent of police infiltration and whether future moves are already anticipated remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Code of Detachment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Neil enforces emotional detachment as a survival mechanism across personal and professional relationships, culminating in his failed attempt to transcend it with Eady. This arc traces his ideological rigidity, the erosion of human connection, and the ultimate collapse of his hope for intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Neil asserts emotional detachment as a necessary principle to maintain control and survival in a criminal life.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Neil stands alone, having severed all ties, yet remains emotionally unfulfilled as Eady fails to reciprocate his vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fundamental incompatibility between Neil's need for control and his desire for authentic connection remains unresolved, undermining his ability to truly escape his past.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Planning to Collapse: The Heist Unravels
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Neil's meticulous planning for the bank heist progresses from reconnaissance to execution, only to collapse due to unforeseen betrayals, crew instability, and police intervention. This arc charts the disintegration of control despite disciplined preparation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Neil leads a coordinated team through careful reconnaissance and planning, confident in operational precision.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The heist ends in violent failure, with the crew scattered, the money abandoned, and Neil on the run.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fragility of even the most disciplined criminal operation in the face of human unpredictability and external surveillance remains a critical vulnerability.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Cleansing the Past: Severing Ties
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Neil systematically eliminates threats and emotional anchors from his past—Waingro, Van Zant, and finally his criminal network—through calculated severance. Each act reinforces his autonomy but deepens his isolation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Neil identifies internal and external liabilities that threaten the success and secrecy of his operations.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Neil stands alone, having cut every operational and emotional tether, symbolically burning the last bridge to his former life.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While external threats are neutralized, the psychological cost of perpetual severance and the risk of retaliation from those cast out remain latent dangers.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Duality of Freedom: Escape vs. Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Neil's desire to escape his criminal identity and start anew with Eady clashes with his inability to offer emotional openness. This arc explores the tension between physical freedom and emotional captivity, culminating in a failed bid for redemption through love.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Neil proposes to Eady, seeking to transform his life through love and flight to a new world.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite physical escape being within reach, emotional distance and Neil's controlling instincts destroy the relationship, leaving him isolated even in freedom.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether true freedom is possible without emotional vulnerability—and whether Neil is capable of it—remains unanswered.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Professional vs. The Pursuer
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Neil's awareness of and engagement with Detective Vincent Hanna evolves from intelligence gathering to direct confrontation, establishing a fatalistic duel between two men defined by their professions. This arc frames their conflict as both tactical and existential.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Neil learns of Hanna's pursuit and assesses him as a uniquely capable adversary.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Neil engages Hanna in a final firefight, embodying the inevitable clash between hunter and hunted who mirror each other's dedication.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Neil's code of honor and autonomy can withstand a pursuer who understands him better than anyone else remains unresolved until their violent confrontation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Home: Duty Over Domesticity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Vincent Hanna's repeated prioritization of duty over emotional presence accelerates the collapse of his marriage to Justine, revealing a pattern of emotional withdrawal and failed connection.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hanna chooses work over intimacy, leaving Justine feeling rejected.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Hanna's emotional outburst in the kitchen symbolizes the internal collapse of his personal life, with no reconciliation in sight.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The mutual need for connection is overshadowed by Hanna's inability to be present, leaving the marriage irreparably fractured.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Surveillance to Confrontation: The Hunt for McCauley
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hanna's pursuit of Neil McCauley evolves from passive surveillance to active, personal confrontation, marking a complete transformation in operational intensity and personal investment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hanna identifies McCauley as a target and initiates surveillance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Hanna abandons his vehicle and sprints on foot to intercept McCauley in a final, physical showdown.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The success of the apprehension remains uncertain, hinging on Hanna's ability to close the gap in real time.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mirror Images: The Psychological Duel
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hanna and McCauley evolve from hunter and prey into mutual observers who recognize each other as reflections—two disciplined men isolated by their codes, locked in a fatal dance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Neil begins surveilling Hanna, reversing the power dynamic.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The two men meet face-to-face and acknowledge their shared existential isolation and unwavering commitment to their paths.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether this recognition will lead to mutual destruction or a twisted form of respect remains unfulfilled.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Control: Leadership Under Pressure
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hanna's insistence on operational control leads to escalating tension within his team, culminating in authoritarian actions that fracture cohesion and raise ethical concerns.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hanna enforces strict silence during surveillance to maintain stealth.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Hanna destroys the radio and isolates himself, asserting dominance at the cost of team unity and communication.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The breakdown in team trust and inter-agency cooperation threatens the legitimacy and effectiveness of the entire operation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fatherhood Interrupted: The Crisis with Lauren
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hanna's failure to be a reliable father mirrors his professional obsession, but a traumatic event forces him into a moment of raw, protective presence—highlighting the duality of his absence and sudden intensity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Lauren's father figure is absent, paralleling Hanna's emotional distance from his own family.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Hanna physically carries his injured daughter into the hospital, asserting himself as her protector despite institutional resistance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Lauren's trauma and Hanna's own injuries go unaddressed, suggesting deeper familial wounds that remain untreated.
